channel.title: Comments for page "A Kind Of Christmas"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Kind Of Christmas"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think so? Adam is just the latest instance SCP-1551-X.

Also, man, it's been three years, but I only just now got around to upvoting 1551. Great stuff, that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did 1551 get rewritten since this was posted? Or am I missing something in the connection?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I love this story, especially the unexpected connection to 747, which adds a whole new perspective to that article.

And for those wondering what the connection between the Kind Man and SCP-747 is, it's:

+ Spoilers
- Hide

the unexplained note mentioned in SCP-747.

Merry Christmas!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very lovely and I am so happy I found it on the site again. A hard favorite for me! Upvoted as many times as legally possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hhh I can't believe I actually forgot to reply to this for all this time

There is a reference. It's a far less obvious one, but I hope that it'll become a bit more obvious with subsequent repetitions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But the kind man looks nothing like the Warden! This cannot be!

Still, I like this idea. Many warm fuzzies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahh, okay. No, it certainly didn't. It makes sense with 747, at least in the way you've presented it here, it's just the odd article out when you go back and find the reference in the other two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't see one, but Soulless had asked for something about his stuff, and 747 had always made an impression on me, so I added the kind man. There are other articles which explicitly feature him, but none of them felt really right for what I was going for. Hopefully it didn't seem shoehorned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why 747? The kind man doesn't seem to be linked to that one (though I was very surprised and pleased to see I'd missed the connection between the other two articles).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I half-expected my 1810 to show up once I saw the imaginary friend guy that soul wrote. This was very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My headcanon:

Doctor Wondertainment is the inspiration of many, many things. In his first few eons, he was likely a Trickster of sorts, albeit mostly harmless. All in good fun, after all. But as people grew and changed, he did so as well, and decided to cut down on the pranks, except every once and a while. And most certainly on a very particular day after summer has reached its twilight and the season of harvest is beginning. However, as the months turn cold, there comes a day when he waltz wherever he pleases, and goes to those who need the most important gift of all. To remember that they are not alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't entirely get this, but I like it, because Christmas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you the feelings. I need tissues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, the Kind Man is a pretty legit dude. And while this qualifies as a complete work (i.e. one with which I'm reasonably happy), I might be updating this at some point, in light of certain aspects of his kindness.

If it's unclear, the kind man is a background character featured in several of Souless' articles, usually managing to escape the Foundation's notice while doing kind things.
